ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Ascertain The Interests Of The Beneficiaries
Off Calendar May-07-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Lofdahl for John Waycott. Linda Garrett for Troy Waycott.

Ms. Lofdahl stated that the matter needed to be set for trial and that depositions were also needed. Ms. Garrett stated that the trial would take 2 days and that discovery had not been completed. The Court referred the matter to the Department of the Presiding Judge, (signing the order for the same in open court).

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR #11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: B. Mann.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Ascertain The Interests Of The Beneficiaries
Off Calendar Apr-16-2008 Continued To May-07-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Ascertain The Interests Of The Beneficiaries
Off Calendar Mar-26-2008 Continued To Apr-16-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Ascertain The Interests Of The Beneficiaries
Off Calendar Jan-30-2008 Continued To Mar-26-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Adriano Hrvatin for Norma Lee Cook (Interested Party) also present. Allan Seher for the Trustees. No appearance was made by any party, counsel or interested person. Nancy Lofdahl for John Waycott. Linda Garrett for Troy Waycott. John McCarron apperaed by telephone for Tori Hutchinson.

The parties informed the Court that the matter regarding the attorneys fees had been worked out and read the agreement for the record. The Trustees will reimburse Tori Hutchinson $15,000.00 for attorney's fees and $3,000.00 for costs. Mr. McCarron will submit the order as to form regarding this matter. In addition, the Court was informed that the matter regarding Norma Lee Cook had also settled, Mr. Hrvatin will file an addendum to the settlement regarding this matter. The dispute between John and Troy has not fully settled. The Court referred this matter to Pro Bono Mediation, (signing the order for the same in open court). Mediation is to be completed by March 19, 2008. Status on mediation is set for March 26, 2008 at 9:00 a.m. Mr. Seher does not have to participate with the mediation nor appear at the next hearing. Mr. McCarron is excused from all other appearances.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR# 11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Lewis.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Appearance Of Troy Waycott
Off Calendar Dec-04-2007 Continued To Jan-11-08 At 08:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John McCarron for Tori Hutchinson. Johanna Hartwig for Norma Cook. Nancy Lofdahl for Jon Waycott. Allen Seher for Trustees. Troy Waycott in Pro Per.

Ms. Lofdahl stated that her client was the most effected, that nothing was filed by Troy Waycott, and that an appearance was not sufficient. Mr. Waycott stated that it took one month to receive notice, that he was represented by counsel, but he was not able to attend todays hearing, and requested a continuance. Ms. Lofdahl stated that she did not send a copy of the opposition to Troy, because she did not know that he was an interested party. The matter was continued to January 11, 2008 for Troy Waycott's attorney (Nancy Hinshaw) to contact all counsel. The Court directed Mr. Seher to make partial distibution.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Rich Lee.

Petition By Benificiary Tori L Hutchinson For Attorneys Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Dec-04-2007 Continued To Jan-11-08 At 08:30 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Reset-Petition To Compel Trustee To Redress Breach Of Trust Filed On 8/11/06
Off Calendar Oct-30-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Lofdahl for John Waycott (Beneficiary). Johanna Hartwig for Norma Lee Cook (Interested Party). John McCarran for Tori Hutchinson. Alen Sehre for the Trustee.

Mr. Seher reported that the property had been sold, that partial distribution was made, that the issue with John Waycott and Tori Hutchinson had settled, and that the assignment from John to Troy was still outstanding. Ms. Lofdahl stated that Troy Waycott had not made a claim and she wanted the terms of the settlement to go on record. Mr. McCarran raised the issue of notice to Troy. Ms. Lofdahl requested Troy be given a deadline to file a claim. The Court continued the issue regarding Troy to December 4, 2007 and instructed the trustee to give him notice. The Court indicated that Troy must appear. Mr. Seher stated another outstanding issue was the amount to be paid to Ms. Cook. Ms. Hartwig requested that Ms. Cook's interest not be impeaded by the on going dispute. The terms of the settlement agreement between John Waycott and Tori Hutchinson were read into the record. The Court stated that as long as Troy's amount was set aside, the distribution would be allowed. Mr. Seher indicated he would file a Petition for Final Distribution 30 days after resolution of Troy's issue. The Court stated that it was not prepared to rule on the attorney' fees at this time.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: A. Aguerre.

Petition By Benificiary Tori L Hutchinson For Attorneys Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Oct-30-2007 Continued To Dec-04-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Ascertain The Interests Of The Beneficiaries
Off Calendar Oct-15-2007 Continued To Jan-11-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Lynn White specially appearing for Nancy Lofdahl appeared for John Waycott. John McCarron appeared for Tori Hutchindon (Beneficiary). Allen Seher appearing for Petitioners/Trustees. Louana Wigg appearing for Norma Lee Cook.

Mr. McCarron expressed his objection to an evidentiary hearing and that Mr. Waycott's claim had no merit. Mr. White requested an evidentiary hearing to resolve the assignment issue. Mr. Seher reported that the Trustees had the money to be distributed. Ms. Wigg stated that she appeared today only to protect her clients interest in the settlement proceeding. Mr. Seher informed the Court that the distribution to Ms. Cook had been made from escrow. A check was sent to Mr. Hrvatin counsel for Ms. Cook. Mr. McCarron indicated that the evidentiary hearing should take one day. The Court ordered an evidentiary hearing for January 11, 2008 at 9:00am before Judge Dearman. All Counsel should submit briefs regarding all matters and all witnesses should be available.

Judge: Herbert Donaldson. Reporter: Patrick Heard CSR#11055. Bailiff: A. Aguerre. Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Reset-Petition To Compel Trustee To Redress Breach Of Trust Filed On 8/11/06
Off Calendar Sep-18-2007 Continued To Oct-30-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Approval Of Settlmeent Agreement & To Ascertain The Interests Of The Beneficiaries
Off Calendar Sep-27-2007 Written Order Received
MINI MINUTES: Alan Seher appearing for and with petitioners/trustees. One of the trustee Sharon Brownlee is in court. John Waycott, an interested party is in court. Adriano Hrvatin appearing for and with Norma Lee Cook. John McCarron appearing for and with Teri Hutchinson. Court understood Ms. Cook's concern to preserve and enforce her contractual right to an interest in the sale of the real property. There is no opposition for approval of the settlement agreement. Court is prepare to approval the settlement agreement with the exception of ascertain the beneficiaries and reserve the validity of assignment. Mr. Waycott stated he intends to protest the validity of assignment. He requested for continuance so he can bring evidence to court. He has an appointment with an attorney today and he hoped to retain one today. Court agreed to continue it for 30 days. If Mr. Waycott has any opposition or claim, he will have to have that filed and served no later tha 10/5. That issue is continued to 10/15 for status.
Mr. Seher raised the need for tomorrow's status hearing. They have a final settlement and they are moving forward on closing the sale by 2nd week of October. They are working on the date of Ms. Cook's vacating the property and payment to her. Court agreed to continue tomorrow's status hearing to 10/30 before Judge Dearman.
Judge Pro Tem: Dorothy L. McMath. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy Lee.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Reset-Petition To Compel Trustee To Redress Breach Of Trust Filed On 8/11/06
Off Calendar Jul-31-2007 Continued To Sep-18-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Glen Shea, attorney for petitioner,Andriano Hrvatin, attorney for N. Cook, John McCarron
Settlement as to the life estate. Mr. McCarron is not satisfied with the time it is taking to settle the matter. He states he would like the distribution matter and the attorney fees matter resolved. He ask the court to compel other counsel to finish by using a 11950 petition. The court continued the matter to 9/18/07. At which time all matters must be resolved. If not the matters will be moved to Department 1.
JODGE:JOHN DEARMAN, REPORTER:KATY BERSAMIN,CSR#11722, CLERK:LARRISA DOTSON, BALIFF:J. GUITRON

Pet To Compel Trustees To Redress Breach Of Tr.
Off Calendar Apr-13-2007 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Evidentiary Hearing.
Off Calendar Apr-13-2007 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Trial
Off Calendar Apr-03-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Alan Seher appearing for trustee. Adriano Hrvatin appearing for Norma Cook. John McCarron appearing by phone for petitioner. Deposition is not completed with Ms. Cook. There is a new settlement offer but Ms. Cook has not decided. Trial date is set on 4/13. Counsel will let the Court know before the trial date if they have settled or if the trial will take longer than one day.
Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Arlene Howell CSR# 2542. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: F. Smith.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Document Production From Aps
Off Calendar Feb-21-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Alan Seher appearing for trustees. Adriano Hrvatin appearing for Norma Cook. Court has reviewed the package from APS. Petitioner is not in court. Court is reluctant to turn it over because of right of privacy. Life estate issue and elder abuse issue discussed. Mr. Seher requested for redacted records. Court finds the confidentiality issue far outweight the benefit. Request is denied.
Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: J. Peralta.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Document Production From Aps
Off Calendar Feb-14-2007 Continued To Feb-21-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Evidentiary Hearing.
Off Calendar Jan-23-2007 Continued To Apr-13-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Pet To Compel Trustees To Redress Breach Of Tr.
Off Calendar Jan-23-2007 Continued To Apr-13-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Alan Seher appearing for co-trustees. Adriano Hrvatin appearing for Norma Cook. Glen Shea appearing by phone for petitioner. Mediation is not completed. Mr. Hrvatin requested for continuance for the trial date to April due to personal reason. Mr. Shea objected to the continuce. Court is continuing the trial date from 2/9 to 4/13 at 9am. Mr. Seher is having problem getting records from APS. He is requesting the court to set a date for production of documents from APS. Court is setting the document production from APS on 2/14 at 10:30am. Status on trial is set on 4/3 at 10:30am. Mr. Shea is allowed to appear by phone.
Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Mizota.

Pet To Compel Trustees To Redress Breach Of Tr.
Off Calendar Oct-31-2006 Continued To Jan-23-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John McCarron (Counsel for Tori Hutchison); Alan Seher (Counsel for Co-Trustees, Sharon Brownlee and Diana Schnick); Adriano Hrvatin (Counsel for Norma Cook).

After extensive discussion by and among counsel and the Court concerning the status of the case and issues which remain outstanding, the Court (1) referred the matter to pro bono mediation which mediation must be completed not later than January 10, 2007; (2) set the matter for status hearing on January 23, 2007 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204; and (3) set the matter for evidentiary hearing on February 9, 2007 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 (pending the results of the mediation). In the event the evidentiary hearing goes forward, Ms. Cook will bear the burden of proof on the issue of whether a life estate was created in her interest.

Judicial Officer: John Dearman, Judge; Court Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR# 11722; Bailiff: None; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Court Examiner: Christine Nahnsen.

Pet To Compel Trustees To Redress Breach Of Tr.
Off Calendar Oct-10-2006 Continued To Oct-31-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: John McCarron appearing for petitioner. Alan Seher appearing for trustees. Adriano Hrvatin appearing for and with Norma Cook. Mr. Seher is new counsel in the case. He has an accounting from the co-conservator. Court wants him to file the accounting. Mr. McCarron is concerned about the execution of the deed. Mr. Seher said the conservators want to clear title before the property is distributed. Mr. McCarron argued that there is no claim on file on the property. Court wants counsel to get together to talk about the not filed claim. Matter is continued to 10/31. Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Mizota.

Pet To Compel Trustees To Redress Breach Of Tr.
Off Calendar Sep-20-2006 Continued To Oct-10-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: John McCarron appearing for petitioner. Diana Butler Schmich and Sharon Brownlee appearing as co-trustees. Adriano Hrvatin appearing for Norma Lee Cook. The co-trustees has the accounting on a spreadsheet. Court is ordering the co-trustee to prepare an accounting pursuant to the rules. They are to get counsel immediately. Mr. McCarron is concerned about issuance of the deed. Ms. Brownlee informs the Court that she was advised by another counsel that it is not in the best interest to sign the deed. Matter is continued to 10/10. Court will waive the co-trustees appearance if they retain counsel with authority to represent them. Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Teena Paxton, CSR #1558. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Cruz.
